All - please don’t get Flood Zones (A, AE, X) confused with evacuation zones. Flood zones are established by FEMA and indicate flooding potential for each year. If you are in a Flood Zone A or AE, then you are in what is known as as the 100-year flood zone, which means in any year there is a 1% chance your property will be flooded. If you are in a Flood Zone X, then you are in what is known as the 500-year flood zone, which means that in any year there is a 0.5% chance your property will be flooded. These have nothing to do with hurricanes or other storms. Every property in Florida is in a flood zone. The difference is the probability of flooding each year. The Evacuation Zones of A, B, C, D, etc. are established by County Emergency Management and are primarily based on potential storm surge impacts. This is what you need to pay attention to. The Evacuation Zones are not the same as flood zones. If you live in an Evacuation Zone that is ordered to evacuate, then leave. Go the the website of whatever county you live in and go to their Emergency Management Page to see their evacuation zone map and see where your property is located. Everyone stay safe! As Denis Phillips says- run from the water and hide from the wind. FYI - I am a Certified Floodplain Manager and have previously worked as a Director of Planning in Florida local government assisting with emergency management.
